variants.name: Identify, Prioritize and Aggregate Pharma Supply Chain Requirements with GxP Compliance
variants.description: This process involves collecting and prioritizing demand signals across the pharmaceutical supply chain, ensuring compliance with GxP regulations, and maintaining batch traceability, audit trails, and electronic signatures. It also includes validation documentation, deviation management, and a CAPA process to ensure the quality and integrity of pharmaceutical products.
regulatory_requirements: 21 CFR Part 11 electronic signature and record-keeping
variants.notes: All automated decisions must be validated and documented per GxP, with a focus on batch traceability, audit trails, and electronic signatures. The system must also ensure data integrity, security, and compliance with regulatory requirements.

variants.name: Identify, Prioritize and Aggregate Food Supply Chain Requirements
variants.description: Process of collecting and prioritizing demand signals across the food supply chain, considering critical control points, allergen management, and cold chain integrity to ensure food safety and compliance with regulatory frameworks
sector_specific_inputs: cold chain storage and transportation requirements
variants.notes: Food safety decisions at critical control points require validated logic and integration with food safety management systems. Automation should ensure data integrity, accuracy, and compliance with regulatory requirements.

variants.name: Analyze Pharmaceutical Supply Chain Capabilities and Capacity
variants.description: This process involves analyzing the pharmaceutical supply chain's capabilities and capacity to meet demand while ensuring compliance with regulatory requirements such as GxP, GMP, and GDP. It includes evaluating the supply chain's ability to maintain batch traceability, manage audit trails, and implement electronic signatures, as well as validating automated decisions and documenting them according to GxP guidelines.
sector_specific_outputs: capability analysis with risk assessment for pharmaceutical products
CAPA (Corrective and Preventive Action) process documentation
sector_specific_kpis: supplier performance in terms of quality and compliance
capacity utilization rate for critical pharmaceutical manufacturing equipment
regulatory_requirements: 21 CFR Part 11 (Electronic Records and Signatures) compliance
compliance_frameworks: GAMP 5 (Good Automated Manufacturing Practice) for automated systems
ISO 13485 (Medical Devices) for quality management systems
variants.notes: Implementation of this process requires careful consideration of regulatory requirements and validation of automated decisions. The AI agent should be trained on pharmaceutical industry data and be able to handle complex validation and documentation requirements.

variants.name: Analyze Food Supply Chain Capabilities and Capacity with Food Safety Considerations
variants.description: This process analyzes the food supply chain's capabilities and capacity to meet demand while ensuring compliance with food safety regulations and managing critical control points, allergen risks, and cold chain integrity
sector_specific_kpis: percentage of suppliers with valid food safety certifications
number of critical control points with validated logic
variants.notes: Implementation requires integration with food safety management systems and consideration of validated logic for critical control points. AI agents should be trained on food safety regulations and industry best practices to ensure accurate decision-making.

variants.name: Determine Pharma Supply Chain Inventory Policy
variants.description: This process determines the inventory policy for pharmaceutical products, taking into account batch traceability, expiration dates, and regulatory requirements to ensure compliance with GxP, GMP, GDP, and ICH Q10 guidelines
sector_specific_outputs: reorder points with consideration of lead times and shelf life
regulatory_requirements: 21 CFR Part 11 electronic signature and record-keeping
compliance_frameworks: GCP for clinical trial supply chain management
variants.notes: All automated decisions must be validated and documented per GxP, with a focus on batch traceability, electronic signatures, and audit trail maintenance. Deviation management and CAPA processes must be integrated into the inventory management system

variants.name: Determine Food Supply Chain Inventory Policy with Food Safety Considerations
variants.description: This process determines the inventory policy for food products, taking into account food safety requirements, such as critical control points, allergen management, and cold chain integrity, to minimize costs and ensure compliance with regulatory frameworks
sector_specific_outputs: inventory policy with food safety considerations
reorder points with buffer stock for food safety emergencies
sector_specific_kpis: percentage of inventory meeting food safety requirements
supplier compliance rate with food safety regulations
variants.notes: Food safety decisions at critical control points require validated logic and integration with food safety management systems. Automation should ensure data integrity, traceability, and compliance with regulatory requirements.

variants.name: Determine Pharma Supply Chain Transportation Policy
variants.description: This process determines the transportation policy for pharmaceutical products, ensuring compliance with regulatory requirements such as GxP, GMP, GDP, and ICH Q10, while maintaining batch traceability, audit trails, and validation documentation
sector_specific_kpis: delivery time to patient or distribution center
regulatory_requirements: ICH Q10 for pharmaceutical quality systems
21 CFR Part 11 for electronic records and signatures
variants.notes: All automated decisions must be validated and documented per GxP, with a focus on batch traceability, audit trails, and electronic signatures. The system must also ensure that all transportation protocols are qualified and validated to prevent temperature excursions and ensure product stability

variants.name: Determine Food Supply Chain Transportation Policy
variants.description: Process of determining transportation policy to meet demand, minimize costs, and ensure food safety and quality in the food supply chain, considering critical control points, allergen management, and cold chain integrity
variants.notes: Food safety decisions at critical control points require validated logic and real-time monitoring. Automation should ensure data integrity, security, and compliance with regulatory requirements. Temperature control, allergen management, and recall procedures must be integrated into the transportation policy.

variants.name: Manage Pharmaceutical Supply Chain Risk
variants.description: This process identifies and mitigates supply chain risks specific to the pharmaceutical industry, ensuring compliance with regulatory frameworks such as GxP, GMP, GDP, GCP, and ICH Q10. It involves assessing risks associated with batch traceability, audit trails, electronic signatures, validation documentation, deviation management, and CAPA processes.
sector_specific_outputs: risk assessment reports with focus on patient safety
mitigation plans for supply chain disruptions impacting drug availability
CAPA plans for addressing deviations and non-conformities
sector_specific_kpis: percentage of batches released on time
regulatory_requirements: ICH Q10 for pharmaceutical quality systems
21 CFR Part 11 for electronic records and signatures
compliance_frameworks: ISO 13485 for medical device quality management
variants.notes: All automated decisions must be validated and documented per GxP, with a focus on data integrity and audit trails. Electronic signatures and validation documentation are crucial for compliance. The CAPA process must be integrated into the risk management framework to ensure timely and effective corrective actions.

variants.name: Manage Food Supply Chain Risk
variants.description: Process of identifying and mitigating supply chain risks specific to food safety, quality, and regulatory compliance, including critical control points, allergen management, and cold chain integrity
sector_specific_kpis: percentage of suppliers with valid food safety certifications
number of critical control points with validated logic
variants.notes: Food safety decisions at critical control points require validated logic and automation to ensure accuracy and compliance. Integration with ERP, CRM, and supply chain management systems is crucial for real-time monitoring and decision-making.

notes: Architect review — Agentic Zero Pioneer Team
